benchmarks.name: Old Hebrew (Biblical)
benchmarks.notes: 22 consonant signs; high token/sign ratio from large corpus

benchmarks.name: Phoenician
benchmarks.source: Literature (Daniels & Bright 1996; cross-validated with NW Semitic cognates)
benchmarks.notes: Typical NW Semitic abjad entropy range 4.1–4.6

benchmarks.name: Proto-Sinaitic
benchmarks.notes: Small corpus (~100 tokens); H1 estimate from sign count and Zipf fit

benchmarks.name: Ugaritic (Baal Cycle)
benchmarks.notes: 30 consonant signs; close relative of Hebrew, used as proxy

benchmarks.name: Meroitic
benchmarks.notes: 23 signs; some vowel notation; borderline alphabetic–syllabic

benchmarks.name: Indus Script
benchmarks.notes: ~400 distinct signs but high-frequency core ~50–75; entropy in syllabic range

benchmarks.name: Old Persian Cuneiform
benchmarks.notes: ~36 syllabograms + 5 logograms + word divider; transitional script

benchmarks.name: NW Semitic Test1 (Fuls corpus)
benchmarks.notes: THIS STUDY — 101 words, 331 tokens; full sign inventory present

benchmarks.name: Cypriot Syllabary (Classical)
benchmarks.source: Literature (Masson 1983; Chadwick 1990); H1 estimated from sign frequency
benchmarks.notes: Classical Greek written in ~55 signs; precursor type to Linear B

benchmarks.name: Linear B (Mycenaean Greek)
benchmarks.notes: 87 syllabograms + logograms; 69 distinct in our corpus; H1 from Chadwick 1990

benchmarks.name: Sumerian Cuneiform (Early Dynastic)
benchmarks.source: Literature (Daniels & Bright 1996; Rao et al. 2009 comparison)
benchmarks.notes: Large inventory; lower H1 than theoretical max due to Zipf concentration

benchmarks.name: Classical Chinese
benchmarks.source: Literature (Rao et al. 2009; information-theoretic studies of Chinese)
benchmarks.notes: Reference logographic extreme; very high entropy from large inventory

conclusion: The NW Semitic test1 corpus has H1=5.607 bits and 78 distinct signs. Both metrics place it firmly in the syllabic tier (H1=4.65–7.80, signs 23–800), well outside the alphabetic/abjad range (H1=4.19–4.65, signs 22–30) and far below the logographic threshold (H1>5.35). Classification: SYLLABIC with HIGH confidence.
